response: The area at Stage 1 is the area of a 3x3 square, which is \(3^2 = 9\) square inches. At each successive stage, a 3x3 square is added. So, the area added at Stage n is \(3^2 \times n\). Therefore, the total area at Stage 6 is the sum of the areas added at each stage from 1 to 6. This is \(9 + 9 \times 2 + 9 \times 3 + 9 \times 4 + 9 \times 5 + 9 \times 6 = 9 \times (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6) = 9 \times 21 = 189\) square inches. </think> <answer> 189 </answer>

After each stage, the length of the rectangle increases by 3 inches and the width remains constant at 3 inches. So after 6 stages, the length will be 3 * 6 = 18 inches, and the width will still be 3 inches. The area of the rectangle will be length * width = 18 * 3 = 54 square inches. </think> <answer> 54 </answer>

The area of a 3'' by 3'' square at each stage adds to the previous area by 9 square inches. So, at Stage 1, the area is 9 square inches. At Stage 2, the area is 9 + 9 = 18 square inches. At Stage 3, the area is 18 + 9 = 27 square inches, and so on. The area at Stage 6 will be 9 times the number of squares added, which is 6. So the area at Stage 6 is \(9 \times 6 = 54\) square inches. </think> <answer> 54 </answer>

The Stage $n$ rectangle has a length of $3n$ inches and a width of 3 inches. The area at Stage $n$ is $3n \times 3 = 9n$ square inches. So, the area at Stage 6 is $9 \times 6 = 54$ square inches. </think> <answer> 54 </answer>
